clinical_study.brief_title: Extended Adjuvant Ribociclib After Chemotherapy in HR+/HER2- Node-Positive Early Breast Cancer (EXTEND-RIB)
clinical_study.official_title: A Phase 2 Randomized Trial of Extended Adjuvant Ribociclib Following Completion of Adjuvant Chemotherapy in Hormone Receptor-Positive, HER2-Negative, Node-Positive Early Breast Cancer With High Genomic Risk

brief_summary.textblock: This trial evaluates whether adding ribociclib (a CDK4/6 inhibitor) as extended adjuvant therapy following completion of standard adjuvant chemotherapy improves invasive disease-free survival in patients with hormone receptor-positive, HER2-negative, node-positive early breast cancer who have high genomic risk as defined by Oncotype DX Recurrence Score >= 26.

detailed_description.textblock: Patients with HR+/HER2- early breast cancer who are node-positive and have a high Oncotype DX Recurrence Score (>= 26) remain at elevated risk of distant recurrence despite completion of adjuvant chemotherapy and endocrine therapy. CDK4/6 inhibitors such as ribociclib have demonstrated benefit in the metastatic setting and early results from the NATALEE trial suggest adjuvant CDK4/6 inhibition may reduce relapse risk. This study randomizes patients who have completed adjuvant chemotherapy (taxane- and/or anthracycline-containing regimens) to ribociclib 400 mg/day (3 weeks on/1 week off) plus standard adjuvant endocrine therapy versus standard adjuvant endocrine therapy alone. Treatment continues for 3 years or until disease recurrence or unacceptable toxicity. Primary endpoint is invasive disease-free survival (iDFS) at 5 years. Key secondary endpoints include distant relapse-free survival, overall survival, and safety/tolerability.

secondary_outcome.measure: Safety and tolerability as assessed by NCI CTCAE v5.0
secondary_outcome.time_frame: During treatment period, up to 3 years

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria: - Histologically confirmed invasive breast carcinoma, hormone receptor-positive (ER >= 1% and/or PR >= 1% by IHC), HER2-negative (IHC 0 or 1+, or FISH non-amplified) - AJCC 8th edition pathologic Stage IIA, IIB, or IIIA (pT1-3, N1-2, M0) - Node-positive disease (at least 1 pathologically confirmed lymph node metastasis) - Oncotype DX Recurrence Score >= 26 (high risk) - Must have completed adjuvant chemotherapy with a taxane- and/or anthracycline-containing regimen (e.g., TC, AC-T, ddAC-T) within 12 months prior to enrollment - Currently receiving or planning to initiate standard adjuvant endocrine therapy (aromatase inhibitor or tamoxifen +/- ovarian suppression) - Age >= 18 years - ECOG Performance Status 0-1 - Adequate bone marrow function: ANC >= 1500/mm3, platelets >= 100,000/mm3, hemoglobin >= 9 g/dL - Adequate hepatic function: total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN, AST/ALT <= 2.5x ULN - Adequate renal function: creatinine clearance >= 30 mL/min - Adequate cardiac function: QTcF <= 450 ms at baseline ECG, LVEF >= 50% - No evidence of distant metastatic disease (M0) confirmed by standard staging workup - Women of childbearing potential must agree to use adequate contraception - Signed informed consent
Exclusion Criteria: - Metastatic (Stage IV) breast cancer - HER2-positive breast cancer - Triple-negative breast cancer (ER-, PR-, HER2-) - Oncotype DX Recurrence Score < 26 - Prior treatment with any CDK4/6 inhibitor (palbociclib, ribociclib, abemaciclib) - Active or prior documented autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment - Known uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia or clinically significant cardiovascular disease - Active hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh B or C) - Concurrent malignancy other than adequately treated non-melanoma skin cancer or cervical carcinoma in situ - Pregnancy or breastfeeding - Known hypersensitivity to ribociclib or any of its excipients - Inability to swallow oral medications - Participation in another interventional clinical trial within 30 days of enrollment

intervention.description: Ribociclib 400 mg orally once daily on a 3-weeks-on/1-week-off schedule for up to 3 years, administered concurrently with standard adjuvant endocrine therapy (aromatase inhibitor or tamoxifen +/- ovarian suppression per treating physician discretion).
Standard adjuvant endocrine therapy alone (aromatase inhibitor or tamoxifen +/- ovarian suppression) for up to 5-10 years per standard of care guidelines.
